summary:
The patient exhibits classic signs of pulmonary-renal syndrome due to anti-basement membrane disease, characterized by pulmonary hemorrhage, hematuria, and fatigability. Linear IgG staining on immunofluorescence is the definitive histologic feature linking these manifestations to Goodpasture's syndrome, making it the most accurate choice among the options.  

\boxed{C}
